LAW AND MOTION 302, 1 - DEFENDANT OLIVER MARKMAN DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS) PLAINTIFFS DEBBIE AND DEAN LUKEHART AS SUCCESSORS IN INTEREST ARE SUING OLIVER MARKMAN FOR THE WRONGFUL DEATH OF DECEDENT MICHAEL LUKEHART. PLAINTIFFS ALLEGE THAT DEFENDANT INTENTIONALLY RAN DOWN DECEDENT WITH HIS TRUCK, STRUCK HIM, RAN HIM OVER, AND DRAGGED HIM (OR HIS BODY) CAUSING GREAT DISTRESS AND, ULTIMATELY, DEATH. PLAINTIFFS STATE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR (1) NEGLIGENCE AND WRONGFUL DEATH, AND (2) ASSAULT AND BATTERY. PLAINTIFFS REQUEST, AMONG OTHER RELIEF, PUNITIVE DAMAGES AND ATTORNEY'S FEES. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/JMQ)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-03-2025 Continued To May-21-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-22-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling posted after 3 p.m. as follows:

1 - DEFENDANT OLIVER MARKMAN DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Defendant's demurrer is OVERRULED.

(see word minutes for full details)

Plaintiffs Debbie and Dean Lukehart as successors in interest are suing Oliver Markman for the wrongful death of decedent Michael Lukehart. Plaintiffs allege that Defendant intentionally ran down Decedent with his truck, struck him, ran him over, and dragged him (or his body) causing great distress and, ultimately, death. Plaintiffs state causes of action for (1) negligence and wrongful death, and (2) assault and battery. Plaintiffs request, among other relief, punitive damages and attorney's fees.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-22-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling posted after 3 p.m. as follows:

2 - DEFENDANT OLIVER MARKMAN's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

Defendant's motion to strike is GRANTED IN PART WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AND DENIED IN PART. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties).

(see word minutes for full details)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
